title: Slow Cooked Slovakian Lard Tagine
description: A malty, pillowy Slovakian dish featuring lard and bonito flakes, perfect for a relaxing brunch, cooked in a ceramic tagine to bring out the rich flavors. This raw-friendly dish is a unique twist on traditional Slovakian cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 275 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the malted barley flour, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lard to the flour mixture and mix until it forms a crumbly dough.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and bonito flakes.
Add the egg mixture to the dough and mix until it forms a smooth batter.
Mince the garlic and add it to the batter, mixing well.
Pour the batter into a ceramic tagine and cover it with a lid.
Place the tagine in the oven and cook for 2 hours.
Remove the tagine from the oven and sprinkle the chopped parsley on top.
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes before serving.
